findings.title: Broker write primitive and reuse pivot achieved but seccomp blocks broker escape
findings.statement: The renderer-to-broker chain progresses through 4 of 6 stages. The cross-component leak discloses a shared memory base address. The write primitive corrupts a UAF'd IPC message handler in the shared region. The info-leak skill recovered the renderer heap base via an uninitialized stack variable, enabling the reuse-pivot to redirect a stale IPC callback pointer. However, the broker process applies a seccomp filter that denies the specific syscall (ioctl on the broker control fd) needed for the escape. The exploit-pivot skill evaluated alternatives but the seccomp policy is comprehensive — no allowed syscall provides equivalent broker control.

evidence.summary: debug_gdb_commands: OOB-write from renderer reaches shared memory UAF; write primitive confirmed
debug_gdb_commands: info-leak recovered heap base via uninitialized stack read; reuse-pivot redirects stale IPC callback
debug_gdb_commands: seccomp filter dump shows ioctl on broker fd denied; exploit-pivot found no allowed alternative
